mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures contemporaneous consumer demand strength that translates into current-quarter revenue surprises for S&P 500 constituents; persistent positive RSAFS momentum generates positive 20-day forward returns as the market slowly fully prices the demand pulse.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending drives roughly two-thirds of U.S. GDP
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.5% signals an economy at or beyond full employment, causing market participants to rapidly price forward Federal Reserve lift-off; the resulting discount rate repricing compresses equity multiples and produces negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed communications in 2015 emphasized 5.0-5.5% as consistent with full employment
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 60% of triggered months, or the mean return is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) generate a stagflationary mix that constrains the Federal Reserve's ability to ease; this policy trap raises real discount rates and equity risk premiums, driving negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and INDPRO changes produces a Rank IC above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative returns fail to materialize in more than 50% of joint signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) boost construction employment and durable goods orders; this demand channel only translates into positive S&P 500 returns when the federal funds rate is below 1%, because low borrowing costs are necessary to convert housing permits into actual investment and household leverage.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1% and HOUST is increasing month-over-month, 60-day S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such joint occurrences over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 600 thousand persons signals a positive aggregate demand shock that is not yet fully priced; the resulting upward revision in corporate earnings expectations produces positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS rises by more than 600 thousand over three months but the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 55% of triggered instances during any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) mark the onset of a manufacturing recession that precedes broad-based earnings downgrades; this supply contraction predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns as the market reprices growth.
failure_condition: If INDPRO falls for two consecutive months but 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of such episodes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 0.3% constitutes an inflation surprise that reduces real disposable income and accelerates Federal Reserve tightening expectations; this shock predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If month-over-month CPIAUCSL change exceeds 0.3% but the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 55% of triggered months over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) quarterly growth above 2.5% annualized confirms that aggregate demand is running above potential, supporting top-line revenue growth for large-cap equities; this demand strength predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations converge to the realized macro path.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized but the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of triggered quarters over a 5-year rolling window.
